cases.expected_note: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/address-function-d0c26c0d-3991-446b-8de4-ab46431d4f89 -- abs_num default is 1 (absolute); row 2, column 3 = C, giving $C$2

cases.description: abs_num=1: absolute row and absolute column

cases.expected_note: Per the ADDRESS docs' abs_num table, mode 2 locks the row with $ but leaves the column floating
Mode 3 is the mirror of mode 2: column locked with $, row left relative

cases.description: abs_num=4: fully relative, no dollar signs
sheet_text argument prefixes the result with a sheet-qualified reference
